explanation: The t_learner_ridge+hurdle ensemble with gap_weights achieves the highest holdout lower CI (0.100) among all combinations, representing a 10.9% improvement over the best single model (hurdle at 0.0902). This combination is optimal for several reasons: (1) Both models are top performers in CV (0.0964 and 0.0898 point estimates), (2) They use fundamentally different approaches - Ridge regression vs. hurdle modeling - suggesting low correlation and high diversity, (3) Nearly equal weights (50.3%/49.7%) indicate both contribute meaningfully without one dominating, (4) Gap-based weighting (based on CV confidence interval widths) provides a principled approach that accounts for model uncertainty, and (5) The holdout performance significantly exceeds CV expectations (0.100 vs CV lower CIs of 0.0713 and 0.0634), suggesting genuine complementarity rather than overfitting.
alternatives_considered: Single hurdle model (0.0902): Strong performer but ensemble provides 10.9% improvement in lower CI
Equal weights strategy (0.0895): Slightly underperforms gap_weights, suggesting differential weighting adds value
CI weights strategy (0.0984): Very close to gap_weights but slightly lower, gap-based approach better captures model stability
Single t_learner_ridge (0.075): Significantly underperforms its ensemble contribution, confirming value of diversity
Larger ensembles with all models: Results show they collapse to the same 2-model combination, indicating other models add noise rather than signal
overfitting_assessment: Low overfitting risk. Multiple indicators suggest robust generalization: (1) The improvement over single models is substantial (10.9%) and consistent across similar weighting strategies (gap_weights, ci_weights both ~0.098-0.100), (2) Both component models have reasonable CV performance with moderate gaps (0.025 and 0.026), (3) The near-equal weighting prevents over-reliance on any single model's idiosyncrasies, (4) The fact that equal weights also perform well (0.0895) suggests the combination is stable and not sensitive to exact weight calibration, and (5) Model diversity (linear Ridge vs. distributional Hurdle) provides complementary perspectives that likely capture different aspects of treatment heterogeneity. The holdout boost appears genuine rather than noise-fitting.
